title: Cryptocurrency seed phrases (mnemonics) should not be disclosed
description: BIP-39 mnemonic seed phrases are the master backup for hierarchical deterministic (HD) wallets. A seed phrase can derive every private key in a wallet, giving complete and permanent control over all associated cryptocurrency addresses and assets across all blockchains. Exposing a seed phrase is equivalent to giving away the entire wallet forever.
recommendation: Remove seed phrases from source code immediately. Seed phrases should only exist on paper in physical secure storage or in hardware wallets. Never store them digitally. Transfer all assets to a new wallet with a new seed phrase immediately if exposed.
impact: Complete and permanent loss of all cryptocurrency assets across all blockchains derivable from the seed phrase, including ETH, BTC, and all EVM-compatible chains.

compliant: # Seed phrases must never appear in source code
# Use hardware wallets or HSMs for production key management

checks.message: Cryptocurrency BIP-39 mnemonic seed phrase detected. Seed phrases are the master key to hierarchical deterministic wallets and can derive every private key for all associated addresses across all blockchains. Exposing a seed phrase gives attackers permanent, complete control over all cryptocurrency assets in the wallet including ETH, BTC, and all tokens. Transfer ALL assets to a new wallet with a new seed phrase immediately. This exposure cannot be remediated — the seed phrase must never be used again.

Cryptocurrency seed phrase detected in variable assignment. A mnemonic phrase assigned to a variable in source code represents a catastrophic security failure. BIP-39 seed phrases encode the master private key for entire HD wallets. Exposing this phrase gives permanent access to all funds across every address the wallet has ever generated. Transfer all assets to a completely new wallet immediately and treat this seed phrase as permanently burned.
